mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future equity cash flows, directly depressing S&P 500 valuations and generating negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of months following rate hikes.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5%, labor market slack reduces corporate wage pressures and signals weakening consumer demand, causing S&P 500 forward returns to turn negative over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered months, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in a multivariate regression controlling for market volatility.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates synchronized domestic demand growth, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in >55% of quarters, or the interaction term in a regression model is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production signals immediate supply-side contraction and inventory drawdowns, triggering short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of observations during late-cycle expansions, or the signal reverses direction during early-cycle phases without regime adjustment.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate surpasses 5.0%, borrowing costs for corporations and consumers become restrictive enough to compress equity multiples, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns exceed +0.5% in >50% of triggered months, or the threshold effect vanishes when controlling for inflation expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total employment combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 60-day returns in >55% of quarters, or the interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample prediction accuracy by at least 10% over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Higher housing starts reflect strong residential investment and construction sector activity, which immediately boosts market sentiment and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of months following housing starts increases, or the signal shows zero predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) in a walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production below 110 indicates a supply-side bottleneck that forces firms to cut output and delay capital expenditures, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month during recovery phases.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of triggered months during post-shock recoveries, or the signal loses predictive significance when inflation is above 3%.
